content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the full branch delta against ca4d23414de261e5b7d9716c8c608569045ab18d, including `src/adapters/worktree.rs`, `tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs`, and the `result` untracking change. I found one high-severity correctness/safety regression in how checkpoint trees handle tracked ignored/assume-unchanged paths; no other in-scope issues stood out.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `build_checkpoint_tree` now removes tracked ignored/assume-unchanged paths from the checkpoint tree entirely, so the checkpoint commit records them as deletions instead of freezing their previously tracked contents.
affected_files:
- src/adapters/worktree.rs:218-223
- src/adapters/worktree.rs:520-548
- src/adapters/worktree.rs:600-601
- tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:347-660
remediation_hint: After `git add -A`, restore ignored/assume-unchanged paths to their current real-index/parent-tree entry instead of `update-index --force-remove` whenever the path is still tracked there; only remove paths that are already absent from the real index (for example after an explicit `git rm --cached`).
   Rationale: Because `create_checkpoint` immediately makes the checkpoint tree the new HEAD and rollback later uses `reset --hard`, this implementation turns any still-tracked ignored path into an unrelated deletion in branch diffs and can delete that file from disk during rollback/resume. The bead goal was to stop leaking local mutations, not to rewrite tracked files out of the branch history.
